notes.note: Causa regia: the question of restoring Ptolemy XII Auletes, the exiled king of Egypt, to his throne --- the commission Lentulus Spinther, as governor of Cilicia, hoped to receive. It dominates the whole Lentulus correspondence of 56 BC.

Promulgatio: the formal posting of a bill before the people. This Cato is C. Porcius Cato, the tribune of 56 BC (not M. Cato Uticensis); his bill aimed at stripping Lentulus of the Egyptian command, which Cicero feared would divert and inflame the whole affair.

notes.anchor: transferred to the man to whom it is by now thought practically transferred
notes.note: The rival for the Egyptian command was Pompey, whom many in Rome wanted entrusted with restoring the king. Cicero, working for Lentulus, dreads seeing the charge slip to Pompey.
Quintus Selicius, an agent of Lentulus at Rome whom Cicero uses for everything touching the Egyptian business, praising him as the most prudent and loyal of Lentulus's men.
